au2_global_derivative_norms_and_tail.conclusion_scope: exact-rational certification of all six global AU.2 derivative norms and the boundary-aware p^-5 continuum tail envelope

origin.conclusion_scope: exact-rational regular-origin bounds for the six third-optical-derivative L1 contributions, derived from the AU.1 Volterra identity without differentiating its L-infinity remainder

spectral_ledger.claim_boundary: AU.2 certifies all six global third-derivative L1 norms and their exact continuum tail envelope from one AU.1 solution. AU.3 finite-frequency quadrature and global Q0, Q1, Q2, G, and M1 remain open.

M_0^A.provenance: exact-rational sum of the regular-origin Volterra-Lie bound and the positive-radius AU.1 Newton-tube interval-jet upper sum

M_0^W.provenance: exact-rational sum of the regular-origin Volterra-Lie bound and the positive-radius AU.1 Newton-tube interval-jet upper sum

M_1^A.provenance: exact-rational sum of the regular-origin Volterra-Lie bound and the positive-radius AU.1 Newton-tube interval-jet upper sum

M_1^W.provenance: exact-rational sum of the regular-origin Volterra-Lie bound and the positive-radius AU.1 Newton-tube interval-jet upper sum

M_2^A.provenance: exact-rational sum of the regular-origin Volterra-Lie bound and the positive-radius AU.1 Newton-tube interval-jet upper sum

M_2^W.provenance: exact-rational sum of the regular-origin Volterra-Lie bound and the positive-radius AU.1 Newton-tube interval-jet upper sum

endpoint.conclusion_scope: directed AU.2 endpoint constants inherited from one closed Newton tube with certified monotonicity, negative wall slope, and positive finite inertia
endpoint.inertia_provenance: the unique nonlinear hard-wall solution in the Newton ball is strictly decreasing, has strictly negative wall slope, and has finite strictly positive dimensionless rotor inertia
endpoint.wall_slope_provenance: the unique nonlinear hard-wall solution in the Newton ball is strictly decreasing, has strictly negative wall slope, and has finite strictly positive dimensionless rotor inertia

au2_positive_derivative_norms.conclusion_scope: exact-rational upper bounds for the six third-optical-derivative L1 contributions on the positive-radius Newton-tube cells only; the regular-origin contribution remains required
